title: cornus sericea subsp. occidentalis
intro: Cornus sericea subsp. occidentalis, also known as Red Osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its red-stemmed branches and white flowers that turn into blue berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating diarrhea. The health benefits of Cornus sericea subsp. occidentalis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
